mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, signaling earnings acceleration that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, yielding a negative directional accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates compress equity multiples unless offset by strong employment growth; when FEDFUNDS rises and PAYEMS remains above 142 million, the labor market resilience supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising rates and strong employment, indicating rate sensitivity dominates labor resilience.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically lead equity markets higher over a 20-day window as construction activity stimulates related corporate earnings.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 235.0, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces central bank hawkishness, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 235.0, indicating markets price in inflation tolerance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and weak retail sales signals a structural shift where wage gains are not translating to consumption, warning of earnings deceleration that reduces S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of walk-forward tests, showing employment and consumption remain positively correlated.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain stress, industrial production growth outpaces retail sales, indicating inventory drawdowns and pricing power that boost corporate margins and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > RSAFS growth in trailing 3 months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth, indicating inventory cycles do not drive equity pricing in this regime.
